name: Monkfish with Ginger Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/4 lb Julienned vegetable (i.e., cucumber, onions, carrot or celery)
Peeled, sliced ginger, set aside in a bowl of water
17 oz White wine, approximately 34 oz Water
recipeInstructions:
This recipe comes from the Japanese consulate and is not a traditional Japanese dish. It uses cream, butter, thyme, and white pepper—ingredients that are not typically found in traditional Japanese cuisine.
From Hisashi Vakada, Japanese Consulate.
1. Saute the vegetables in a small amount of oil. Season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
2. Cut the monkfish into 4 pieces, one for each person. Season the fish with salt and pepper; dip into flour and shake lightly. Put the butter in a baking pan, add the fish, and bake in a 350°F oven for about 10 minutes. Set aside and keep warm.
3. Combine white wine and fish stock in a saucepan. Place over medium-high heat and reduce until only a small amount of liquid remains in the bottom of the pan. Turn off the heat.
4. Add heavy cream to the pan. Cook over low heat for 3 to 5 minutes. Add drained ginger to the sauce.
5. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Pour the sauce over the fish before serving.
6. Serves 4.
FISH STOCK:
1. Melt butter in a stockpot; add white fish bones and saute. Be careful that the bones do not stick or burn. Add onion slices and white wine. Turn heat to high and reduce liquid by half, skimming the top to remove residue.
2. Turn the heat to low. Add water and herbs. Simmer for 2 to 3 minutes. Strain and reserve the stock. Discard the bones.
